title: Invoke Ledger operations non-interactively
description: Provide deterministic JSON-oriented process input, output, diagnostics, and error behavior for the public command contract. Implements G5 and UC-LEDGER-006 under C2 and C14.
rationale: Agents must distinguish structured outcomes from diagnostics and must never parse prose to know whether a financial mutation succeeded.
story_want: agents to invoke commands through a stable process contract
story_so_that: automation can handle success and failure deterministically
acceptance_criteria: Given a structured public operation, when valid JSON input is supplied through the documented non-interactive input path, then exactly one structured result is written to stdout and the process exits with the documented success code.

Given an expected validation, conflict, lifecycle, compatibility, cursor, or not-found failure, when the operation completes, then the documented structured error channel contains one machine-readable module-scoped error, diagnostics remain on stderr, and the documented non-zero exit is stable.
Given malformed JSON or unknown fields forbidden by the operation schema, when invocation is attempted, then validation fails before any mutation.
Given a successful or expected-failure invocation, when stderr is inspected, then it contains no transaction description, full account identifier, credential, or serialized request or response body.
security_criteria: Diagnostics and logs contain metadata only, never financial payloads, credentials, or full account identifiers.
